summary: The single biggest lever is closing the repair/card loop at the judge gate. The docs' own accounting (SESSION-2026-07-01-summary.md:57-61) shows ~92% of judged candidates die at the repair stage (7.667 rejected vs 0.667 kept per video), and two structural facts make that gate needlessly lossy: the repair loop can only GROW clips (off_topic/other verdicts dead-end after one judge call, validate.py:173-262), and the context card — the cheapest fix for exactly the missing_prerequisite/unresolved_reference failures that dominate — is generated only AFTER the accept/reject decision (assemble/__init__.py:126 vs :178), while eval judges clip+card (metrics.py:40). So the pipeline gates on a weaker artifact than it ships and measures, simultaneously depressing yield (0.667) and making comprehension 0.333 a number about an unvalidated artifact. Fix: card-as-repair-action + gate on clip+card, add a shrink move, then run the already-planned repair-yield tuning pass using the rejections_* columns. The essential companion is roadmap #6's human calibration set — without it the strict same-family judge (config.py:226,232) that causes the 7.7 rejections is an unanchored ruler, so any yield/comprehension tradeoff tuning is optimizing a proxy. Coverage-class gaps (OCR-off visual videos = 0 clips; anchor clustering narrowing MAX_ANCHORS coverage) and the surfacing gap (cards/hints computed but never rendered) are the next tier: the latter is the cheapest real end-user comprehension win since the artifacts already exist.

gaps.title: Repair gate rejects ~92% of judged candidates (~7.7/video vs 0.667 kept) and the repair loop can only GROW clips
gaps.evidence: docs/SESSION-2026-07-01-summary.md:57-61,180-182 ('0.667 kept + 7.667 repair + 0.333 dedupe per video'; named 'a visible tuning lever'); audit 'Repair loop can only GROW clips' docs/clip-editing-robustness-audit.md:195-199 (off_topic/other match no hint branch, no shrink move, budget burned after one attempt) — re-verified open: backend/pipeline/assemble/validate.py:173-262 builds only additive targets; docs/SESSION-2026-07-01-summary.md:141-143 defers the 'repair-yield tuning pass' to after pkg 2
gaps.why_it_hurts: This IS the yield number: ~7.7 borderline candidates per video die at the repair-stage judge under the strict rubric, leaving 0.667 clips/video. A clip that fails as 'off_topic' or bloated is unrepairable by construction (growth-only), so entire topics covered only by borderline candidates yield zero clips for the user.
gaps.fix_direction: Docs propose: tune min_comprehension_score / repair budget using the new rejections_* eval columns (session summary); audit proposes a shrink repair move (drop lowest-relevance far-from-anchor optional unit on off_topic/near-floor failures, contract-checked before spending the judge call); plus the card-repair branch (next gap).

gaps.title: Context card is never part of the quality gate: pipeline judges clip-only and writes the card afterward, while eval measures clip+card
gaps.evidence: Audit finding 1 [high/L] docs/clip-editing-robustness-audit.md:9-13 (unshipped — not in pkg 1/3 headers). Confirmed live: backend/pipeline/assemble/__init__.py:126 judges with context_card="" and generates the card only at :178 after accept+snap; backend/eval/metrics.py:40 judges WITH context_card. So headline comprehension 0.333 is measured on an artifact (clip+card) the pipeline never validated.
gaps.why_it_hurts: Two direct hits: (a) candidates whose only defect is a missing prerequisite/reference get grown or rejected (feeding the 7.7 rejections/video) when a one-sentence card would have saved them cheaply; (b) shipped cards are unchecked — a duplicative, truncated, or wrong card degrades viewer comprehension and card regressions are invisible because the gate and the metric measure different artifacts.
gaps.fix_direction: Audit proposal: add a card-repair branch to validate_and_repair (generate the card early when failing gates are only prerequisites/references, re-judge with context_card set, accept 'card-completed' clips without growing them) and add one final judge pass on the shipped clip+card so gate and eval align.

gaps.title: Judge validity is unanchored: no human calibration/golden set, same-family judge, and the rubric derives from summarization NLG not the isolation criterion
gaps.evidence: RESEARCH.md:20-29 (self-preference headline; same-Gemini-family judge = 'partial mitigation'; full fix needs different family OR humans), RESEARCH.md:104-108 (open Q4: 'the only real fix is a small human-labeled calibration set'), RESEARCH.md:123 (Tier 3 human calibration set = todo); docs/SESSION-2026-07-01-summary.md:99 (no golden files, gold metrics NaN) and :135-138 (roadmap #6 pairing). Confirmed: backend/config.py:226,232 (JUDGE_PROVIDER='same', JUDGE_MODEL=gemini-2.5-flash-lite).
gaps.why_it_hurts: comprehension 0.333 could be mis-scaled in either direction: a miscalibrated judge simultaneously rejects viewer-understandable clips (killing yield at the 7.7/video gate) and passes confusing ones. Every knob (floor, repair budget, quality weights) is being tuned against an unvalidated proxy, and gold metrics (role_accuracy, anchor_recall, boundary_error, Pk/WindowDiff) are all NaN so segmentation/role quality cannot be graded at all.
gaps.fix_direction: Roadmap #6: human-labeled 'understandable-in-isolation' set built around eval/make_golden.py skeletons (docs call it the only real judge-validity fix); interim strengthening via a cross-family judge (valid Groq key) or gemini-2.5-pro.

gaps.title: Visual-dependent content class yields 0 clips: OCR off by default, no equation recovery, no 'unclippable transcript-only' rule
gaps.evidence: RESEARCH.md:76-88 (Area 4: OCR is the highest-value visual modality, 'OCR_ENGINE="none" by default today', Tier 3 todo; Texo/GLM-OCR/Nougat leads) and RESEARCH.md:101-105 (open Q2: no threshold for when a clip must include the visual; Q3: per-reference on-screen grounding unsolved); docs/SESSION-2026-07-01-summary.md:141 (roadmap #5 queued after pkg 2). Confirmed: backend/config.py:241 OCR_ENGINE default 'none' (MULTIMODAL=1 at config.py:164 gives Gemini keyframe captions only).
gaps.why_it_hurts: A whole genre of input — math/physics/coding videos where the meaning lives on the board or slides (the documented 8uNod 0-clip class) — produces zero clips: missing_visual judge failures are unrepairable because the on-screen equations/text are never recovered, so coverage collapses to talking-head content.
gaps.fix_direction: Roadmap Tier 3: enable OCR and fuse with transcript; try Texo (20M formula→LaTeX) / GLM-OCR / Nougat for equation recovery. The 'when must the visual be included' threshold is unsolved — needs research (RESEARCH.md open Q2).

gaps.title: Comprehension affordances (context cards, 'watch first' hints, titles, quality signals) are computed but never shown to the viewer
gaps.evidence: Audit [high/S] docs/clip-editing-robustness-audit.md:111-121 (cards/prereqs never rendered; verdict/warnings/final_quality stripped by _build_embed_clips); docs/SESSION-2026-07-01-summary.md:109-113 (pkg-2 scope A: 'both generated today but never shown' — not yet built). Confirmed: frontend/src/components/ClipCard.tsx has no context_card/prerequisite/quality rendering; backend/orchestrator.py _build_embed_clips whitelist unchanged.
gaps.why_it_hurts: The pipeline's entire mechanism for isolation comprehension — the 'earlier in the video…' card and prerequisite hints — is paid for in LLM calls, shipped over the wire, and thrown away at render time. End users see strictly worse comprehension than the pipeline already achieved, and cannot triage which of their few clips are the dubious ones.
gaps.fix_direction: Pkg-2 scope A (planned, unbuilt): expose verdict summary/warnings/final_quality/rejections in clip payloads; render cards as a preface, prerequisite chips, and per-clip quality badges.

gaps.title: Best-partial path bypasses the comprehension floor: sub-threshold clips ship unmarked and the judge's score never feeds final_quality
gaps.evidence: Audit [medium/S] docs/clip-editing-robustness-audit.md:309-313 (best-partial ignores min_score; understandable never consulted; final_quality has no verdict.score term so partials land ~0.8, above the 0.45 floor). Confirmed open: backend/pipeline/assemble/validate.py:377 best-partial hard-core gate still live; not addressed by pkg 1/3.
gaps.why_it_hurts: Clips the judge scored well below 0.70 (even understandable=False) ship with no marker — they are the clips dragging measured comprehension to 0.333 while looking healthy in the payload, and the quality floor cannot catch them because the floor and the comprehension gate measure disjoint things.
gaps.fix_direction: Audit proposal: blend verdict.score into completeness_score (e.g. 0.5*boolean-ratio + 0.5*score) or add a secondary floor for partials (score >= min_score - 0.15) plus a 'partial: true' flag surfaced downstream.

gaps.title: Repair/closure span budget (300s) exceeds the physical cut cap (240s); post-judge shrinks still leave overclaiming metadata
gaps.evidence: backend/config.py:123,213 (max_clip_duration_s=240 vs CLOSURE_MAX_SPAN_S=300, comment admits 'refine still caps to max_clip_duration_s'); audit [high/M] docs/clip-editing-robustness-audit.md:75-79 (repair adds result units at the END, exactly what capping chops; only a 0.10 boundary penalty); Post-IC backlog docs/clip-editing-robustness-audit.md:358-361 + docs/SESSION-2026-07-01-summary.md:130-133 (shrink-aware metadata repair, whisper-drift warning, merged grounding recompute — all deferred to pkg 2). Pkg 1 fixed grow-side truthfulness only.
gaps.why_it_hurts: A clip that passed the judge precisely because repair appended the result can ship with the result cut off — the viewer watches a worked example with no answer. Trim/cap shrinks leave unit_ids overclaiming, so cards, prerequisite hints, and eval grounding describe content no longer in the clip.
gaps.fix_direction: Audit proposal: clamp the repair/closure span budget to max_clip_duration_s so repair can't add content the snap will cut; shrink-aware unit_ids helper on trim/cap paths; recompute boundary/final quality after the Whisper pass (pkg-2 D backlog).

gaps.title: Contract/closure semantics unsound: three dead adapter knobs, position-blind precheck, greedy contract inlining starves graph closure
gaps.evidence: Audit [medium/M+S] docs/clip-editing-robustness-audit.md:153-157 (repeatable / max_span_units / close_concept_prerequisites set by 8 adapters, zero consumers — re-verified by grep: no readers outside adapters/), :243-247 (check_contract ignores element position and anchor, so the NEXT problem's setup satisfies 'before'), :249-253 (document-order-greedy step-1 inlining; contract units consume max_extra_units so contract-heavy anchors get zero dependency closure).
gaps.why_it_hurts: Clips splice in the wrong problem statement (a mislabeled setup from 10 minutes earlier), pass the deterministic precheck while missing their actual required element, and contract-heavy anchors ship with no prerequisite closure — producing both judge rejections (feeding the 7.7/video) and shipped clips that confuse viewers in exactly the ways the contracts were designed to prevent.
gaps.fix_direction: Audit proposals: implement the three flags (nearest-only for non-repeatable 'within', thread max_span_units into ClosureBudget, gate the requires-walk on close_concept_prerequisites); pass anchor/order into check_contract; sort required elements nearest-first and give graph closure guaranteed headroom.

gaps.title: No anchor de-clustering: MAX_ANCHORS=12 is consumed by overlapping anchors, crowding out coverage of other video sections
gaps.evidence: Audit [medium/M] docs/clip-editing-robustness-audit.md:159-163 (top-12 by score, no overlap/diversity constraint; each clustered anchor burns full closure + up to 4 judge calls before dedupe collapses them); backend/config.py:208 (MAX_ANCHORS=12); backend/pipeline/assemble/validate.py:352 (verdict cache keyed frozenset(unit_ids) — only exact matches share, and role is ignored).
gaps.why_it_hurts: Adjacent anchors in the same worked example each get judged and repaired only to be merged or dropped at dedupe, while other sections of the video get no anchor at all — topic coverage narrows, judge quota is wasted exactly where repair loops need it, and judge noise arbitrarily picks which twin survives (run-to-run clip flapping).
gaps.fix_direction: Audit proposal: after scoring, suppress anchors sharing a content-map node_id or whose closures overlap >~60% (closure is cheap and deterministic); cap anchors per topic node. Related one-liner: key the verdict cache on (role, frozenset(unit_ids)).

gaps.title: Card generation quality defects: grounding guard leaks, mid-sentence truncation, and no card coverage when the defining clip was dropped
gaps.evidence: Audit [medium/M+S] docs/clip-editing-robustness-audit.md:135-139 (anchor counts as 'earlier context' so zero-information cards pass; fuzz.partial_ratio>=55 lets half-hallucinated two-clause sentences through; relation labels discarded before the writer LLM), :225-228 (40-word cap chops cards mid-sentence, also corrupting eval's per-sentence entailment), :147-151 (best-partial with prerequisites_satisfied=False gets neither card sentence nor hint when the defining clip was floor-dropped). Pkg 1 shipped only the empty-card extractive fallback, not these.
gaps.why_it_hurts: The card is the sole isolation mechanism for referential context: a card that restates the clip's own content, ends in a dangling fragment ('...the notion of'), or omits the one missing prerequisite directly costs viewer comprehension — and since eval judges clip+card, these defects depress the 0.333 headline too.
gaps.fix_direction: Audit proposals: label the anchor separately and drop anchor-only card sentences; per-clause grounding or token_set_ratio>=70 with a length floor; include relation tags in the writer prompt; cap at sentence granularity; append the dropped definer's introducing unit to referential before card generation.

gaps.title: No per-clip fix/restore loop and no stable clip identity: rejected near-misses unrecoverable, prerequisite indices dangle after post-assembly drops
gaps.evidence: Audit [high/L] docs/clip-editing-robustness-audit.md:15-19 (no per-clip re-judge; floor/dedupe drops invisible and un-promotable despite cached Structure making it nearly free) and :213-217,:231-235 (dangling prerequisite_clips + gapped numbering after Whisper-pass drops); Post-IC backlog :358 (Whisper drops escape the ledger); docs/SESSION-2026-07-01-summary.md:114-124,128-133 (pkg-2 B/C/D scope incl. stable clip_id 'also fixes the sequencing-renumber staleness' — planned, not built).
gaps.why_it_hurts: With ~7.7 ledgered rejections per video, good near-miss candidates now exist on record but the user cannot restore, nudge, or re-judge any of them — yield stays pinned at 0.667 even when better alternates were computed. Meanwhile 'watch clip 3 first' can point at a clip that no longer exists, actively breaking sequencing comprehension.
gaps.fix_direction: Pkg-2 B/D (spec'd, unbuilt): stable clip_id; revalidate/restore/nudge/re-export endpoints over the cached Structure + rejection ledger; re-run sequence_clips after the Whisper pass; ledger the Whisper-pass drops via before/after count.

gaps.title: Default embed playback bleeds ~1-2s past the judged boundary and embeddability is never checked
gaps.evidence: Audit [medium/M] docs/clip-editing-robustness-audit.md:171-175 (end=ceil(end)+1 leaks the next sentence; playable_in_embed never read → embed-disabled videos finish 'done' with every clip 'Video unavailable') and :327-331 (tail-pad/uncapped cut_end rewrite). Confirmed open: backend/orchestrator.py:41 still 'int(math.ceil(end)) + 1'; backend/config.py:130,165 OUTPUT_MODE default 'embed'. Not touched by pkg 1/3.
gaps.why_it_hurts: On the default output mode, every clip plays past its sentence-snapped end into the next thought — the sub-second boundary work (snap, direction-safe Whisper picks) is undone at render time; and for embed-disabled videos the user gets a 'done' job whose clips are all unplayable (silent 0 usable clips).
gaps.fix_direction: Audit proposals: drop the +1 (plain ceil, or floor+conditional pad); read playable_in_embed from yt-dlp info (or oEmbed probe on the supadata path) and auto-switch to output_mode=cut or fail actionably.

verified_top.title: Repair gate rejects ~92% of judged candidates (~7.7/video vs 0.667 kept) and the repair loop can only GROW clips
verified_top.evidence: docs/SESSION-2026-07-01-summary.md:57-61,180-182 ('0.667 kept + 7.667 repair + 0.333 dedupe per video'; named 'a visible tuning lever'); audit 'Repair loop can only GROW clips' docs/clip-editing-robustness-audit.md:195-199 (off_topic/other match no hint branch, no shrink move, budget burned after one attempt) — re-verified open: backend/pipeline/assemble/validate.py:173-262 builds only additive targets; docs/SESSION-2026-07-01-summary.md:141-143 defers the 'repair-yield tuning pass' to after pkg 2
verified_top.why_it_hurts: This IS the yield number: ~7.7 borderline candidates per video die at the repair-stage judge under the strict rubric, leaving 0.667 clips/video. A clip that fails as 'off_topic' or bloated is unrepairable by construction (growth-only), so entire topics covered only by borderline candidates yield zero clips for the user.
verified_top.fix_direction: Docs propose: tune min_comprehension_score / repair budget using the new rejections_* eval columns (session summary); audit proposes a shrink repair move (drop lowest-relevance far-from-anchor optional unit on off_topic/near-floor failures, contract-checked before spending the judge call); plus the card-repair branch (next gap).

verdict.corrected_status: high — the repair gate is the pipeline's dominant yield choke (7.7/video rejected vs 0.667 kept) and off_topic/bloated failures are structurally unrepairable (growth-only, budget burned after one judge call); explicitly deferred to pkg 2, not shipped
verdict.reasoning:
REALITY — confirmed open, refutation failed on every axis:

(1) Growth-only repair is verbatim in current code. expand_candidate only ADDS units: backend/pipeline/assemble/validate.py:264 (`chosen = set(cur)`), :265-269 (loop only `chosen.add(t)`), :270-271 (`if chosen == cur: return None`) — no code path ever removes a unit. `grep -rn shrink backend/pipeline/` returns zero hits.

(2) off_topic/other match no hint branch: the kind-hint dispatch at validate.py:222-239 covers only prerequisite/reference/problem/reasoning/result/visual/ground; 'off_topic' and 'other' fall through. The boolean fallbacks at validate.py:241-256 fire only when a boolean is False, so an off_topic verdict with all booleans true yields empty `targets` → validate.py:262-263 returns None → the loop breaks at validate.py:373-374 after ONE judge call, leaving JUDGE_MAX_REPAIR=3 (backend/config.py:217) unspent for that failure class. The only partial mitigation is the best-partial escape at validate.py:377-381, which the measured numbers already include.

(3) Not fixed by any shipped package. docs/superpowers/specs/ contains only the four shipped specs (treeseg-content-map, judge-rubric, clip-integrity-chain, quick-wins) — no shrink/tuning spec. The progress ledger explicitly defers it: .superpowers/sdd/progress.md, PKG 1 completion line: 'Follow-up noted: repair-stage yield (7.7 drops/video under strict rubric judge) is the candidate-throughput lever to revisit; whisper-pass drop ledgering + shrink-aware repair → pkg 2.' docs/SESSION-2026-07-01-summary.md:141-143 likewise schedules 'the repair-yield tuning pass' after pkg 2.

(4) Numbers and defaults unchanged: docs/SESSION-2026-07-01-summary.md:57-61 ('0.667 kept + 7.667 repair + 0.333 dedupe per video ... ~7.7/video under the strict rubric judge — now a visible tuning lever'); threshold untouched at backend/config.py:134 (min_comprehension_score 0.70) and :218 (JUDGE_MIN_SCORE = 0.70). The audit finding is reproduced verbatim at docs/clip-editing-robustness-audit.md ('[medium/M] Repair loop can only GROW clips — off_topic/other verdicts produce zero targets and burn the whole repair budget after one attempt') with no 'shipped' header.

IMPACT — material, not cosmetic. ~92% of judged candidates die at this single stage; 0.667 clips/video is the end-user yield. Nuance: many rejects may be legitimately non-self-contained (the strict judge working as intended), so pure threshold-lowering trades rigor for yield — that part is a tuning question, not a bug. But the structural half of the gap IS a defect: any candidate whose failure mode is 'contains too much / drifted off topic' (tangents pulled in by closure, span-cap-truncated repairs) receives zero effective repair attempts by construction, so whole topics represented only by such candidates produce no clips at all — a direct coverage/yield loss the docs themselves label 'KEY OPEN NUMBERS ... the yield-vs-rigor tuning lever'. Severity high stands: it is the largest single lever on clips-delivered-per-video and both docs and ledger treat it as the top open follow-up.

verified_top.title: Context card is never part of the quality gate: pipeline judges clip-only and writes the card afterward, while eval measures clip+card
verified_top.evidence: Audit finding 1 [high/L] docs/clip-editing-robustness-audit.md:9-13 (unshipped — not in pkg 1/3 headers). Confirmed live: backend/pipeline/assemble/__init__.py:126 judges with context_card="" and generates the card only at :178 after accept+snap; backend/eval/metrics.py:40 judges WITH context_card. So headline comprehension 0.333 is measured on an artifact (clip+card) the pipeline never validated.
verified_top.why_it_hurts: Two direct hits: (a) candidates whose only defect is a missing prerequisite/reference get grown or rejected (feeding the 7.7 rejections/video) when a one-sentence card would have saved them cheaply; (b) shipped cards are unchecked — a duplicative, truncated, or wrong card degrades viewer comprehension and card regressions are invisible because the gate and the metric measure different artifacts.
verified_top.fix_direction: Audit proposal: add a card-repair branch to validate_and_repair (generate the card early when failing gates are only prerequisites/references, re-judge with context_card set, accept 'card-completed' clips without growing them) and add one final judge pass on the shipped clip+card so gate and eval align.

verdict.corrected_status: high — gate judges clip-only (validate.py:138-139, assemble/__init__.py:125-126) while cards are written unjudged afterward (assemble/__init__.py:178) and eval measures clip+card (metrics.py:39-40); costs yield on card-fixable rejections and ships unvalidated cards
verdict.reasoning: Open in code: (1) validate_candidate omits context_card entirely — backend/pipeline/assemble/validate.py:138-139, and judge_clip only includes the card block when non-empty (validate.py:97,106); (2) the post-merge re-judge hardcodes context_card="" — backend/pipeline/assemble/__init__.py:125-126; (3) cards are generated at stage 6 after accept+snap+quality floor (assemble/__init__.py:178) with only the deterministic missing_context_card warning (assemble/__init__.py:33-34, scoring.py:38), never a judge pass on clip+card; (4) repair loop's only remedies are contract fill + expand_candidate, then rejection (validate.py:346-389), and the best-partial keep requires all_references_resolved (validate.py:380), so reference/prerequisite-only failures — the card-fixable class — are grown or rejected, feeding the rejection ledger; (5) eval comprehension judges WITH the card (backend/eval/metrics.py:39-40), so the headline metric measures an artifact the gate never validated. Not fixed by shipped work: PKG1 only shipped the card generation fallback (docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-01-clip-integrity-chain-design.md:108-116; audit header line 4 'card fallback addressed'), and 2026-07-02-quick-wins-design.md / 2026-07-01-judge-rubric-design.md contain no card mentions; the finding remains at docs/clip-editing-robustness-audit.md:9-13 outside the shipped-package headers. Impact is material, not cosmetic: yield loss (clips rejected/over-grown when a one-sentence card suffices) and shipped-card correctness (wrong/duplicative cards go out unchecked, directly degrading viewer comprehension) both hit end-user quality dimensions (comprehension-in-isolation, clips/video). Nuance: since the clip-only gate is stricter, the gate/eval mismatch mostly flatters eval rather than shipping broken clips — but the yield and unvalidated-card effects alone justify high.

verified_top.title: Judge validity is unanchored: no human calibration/golden set, same-family judge, and the rubric derives from summarization NLG not the isolation criterion
verified_top.evidence: RESEARCH.md:20-29 (self-preference headline; same-Gemini-family judge = 'partial mitigation'; full fix needs different family OR humans), RESEARCH.md:104-108 (open Q4: 'the only real fix is a small human-labeled calibration set'), RESEARCH.md:123 (Tier 3 human calibration set = todo); docs/SESSION-2026-07-01-summary.md:99 (no golden files, gold metrics NaN) and :135-138 (roadmap #6 pairing). Confirmed: backend/config.py:226,232 (JUDGE_PROVIDER='same', JUDGE_MODEL=gemini-2.5-flash-lite).
verified_top.why_it_hurts: comprehension 0.333 could be mis-scaled in either direction: a miscalibrated judge simultaneously rejects viewer-understandable clips (killing yield at the 7.7/video gate) and passes confusing ones. Every knob (floor, repair budget, quality weights) is being tuned against an unvalidated proxy, and gold metrics (role_accuracy, anchor_recall, boundary_error, Pk/WindowDiff) are all NaN so segmentation/role quality cannot be graded at all.
verified_top.fix_direction: Roadmap #6: human-labeled 'understandable-in-isolation' set built around eval/make_golden.py skeletons (docs call it the only real judge-validity fix); interim strengthening via a cross-family judge (valid Groq key) or gemini-2.5-pro.

verdict.corrected_status: high — judge validity is still unanchored: golden dir empty (all gold metrics NaN), default judge is same-Gemini-family (config.py:226,232), human calibration set explicitly declared out-of-scope by the shipped judge-rubric package and still 'todo' in the roadmap, while the judge gates every clip at the 0.70 floor and demonstrably swings yield.
verdict.reasoning: REALITY (open, all refutation attempts failed): (1) No golden/calibration set exists — /Users/vincentfeng/Documents/practice/clips/backend/eval/golden/ is empty; gold metrics only computed when golden files exist (backend/eval/run_eval.py:207-211), so role_accuracy/anchor_recall/boundary_error are NaN as claimed (docs/SESSION-2026-07-01-summary.md:99,166); backend/eval/make_golden.py:1-10 only dumps skeletons for a human who has never labeled them. (2) Same-family judge confirmed in live defaults: backend/config.py:226 JUDGE_PROVIDER='same', backend/config.py:232 JUDGE_MODEL='gemini-2.5-flash-lite' vs author gemini-2.5-flash; RESEARCH.md:26-29 calls this 'partial mitigation (same family)... Full fix needs a different family OR humans'; cross-family Groq is opt-in with an invalid key (config.py:222). (3) The shipped judge-rework package explicitly excluded the fix: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-01-judge-rubric-design.md:92 'Out of scope: Human calibration set (#6 — the only true validity measure; this change improves discrimination and stability, not proven validity)'; RESEARCH.md:123 Tier 3 human calibration set = todo; RESEARCH.md:104-108 open Q4 unresolved. CORRECTION to one sub-claim: the shipped rubric text IS the isolation criterion (backend/pipeline/assemble/validate.py:60 'brand-new viewer, watching only this clip', anchored bands :69-74); it is the judge techniques' validation EVIDENCE that comes from summarization NLG (RESEARCH.md Area 2 caveat), a nuance that does not close the gap. IMPACT (material): the judge is simultaneously the production gate (backend/config.py:212 JUDGE_MIN_SCORE=0.70), the repair-loop trigger (validate.py:4), and the eval headline metric (config.py:210 'also the eval comprehension scorer'); RESEARCH.md's own frozen-specs A/B shows judge changes alone move surviving clips 4→2 and mean score 0.483→0.400, so a miscalibrated judge directly changes end-user yield and which clips ship; with all gold metrics NaN, segmentation/role quality is ungradable, so every quality knob is tuned against an unvalidated proxy. Both lenses satisfied → isReal=true, severity high.

verified_top.evidence: RESEARCH.md:76-88 (Area 4: OCR is the highest-value visual modality, 'OCR_ENGINE="none" by default today', Tier 3 todo; Texo/GLM-OCR/Nougat leads) and RESEARCH.md:101-105 (open Q2: no threshold for when a clip must include the visual; Q3: per-reference on-screen grounding unsolved); docs/SESSION-2026-07-01-summary.md:141 (roadmap #5 queued after pkg 2). Confirmed: backend/config.py:241 OCR_ENGINE default 'none' (MULTIMODAL=1 at config.py:164 gives Gemini keyframe captions only).
verified_top.why_it_hurts: A whole genre of input — math/physics/coding videos where the meaning lives on the board or slides (the documented 8uNod 0-clip class) — produces zero clips: missing_visual judge failures are unrepairable because the on-screen equations/text are never recovered, so coverage collapses to talking-head content.
verified_top.fix_direction: Roadmap Tier 3: enable OCR and fuse with transcript; try Texo (20M formula→LaTeX) / GLM-OCR / Nougat for equation recovery. The 'when must the visual be included' threshold is unsolved — needs research (RESEARCH.md open Q2).

verdict.corrected_status: medium — the visual-dependent class does still quality-collapse (fresh run on 8uNod: 2 clips shipped, comprehension_rate 0.0) and the unclippable-threshold rule is genuinely absent, but the claim's core mechanism is overstated: production default already recovers on-screen equations verbatim via Gemini keyframe vision and feeds them to the judge and missing_visual repair, so this is a partially-mitigated gap, not a total 'never recovered' hole
verdict.reasoning:
REALITY — partially open, but the claimed mechanism is overstated. Confirmed-open parts: (1) OCR_ENGINE defaults to "none" (backend/config.py:241) and roadmap #5 (OCR+Texo/GLM-OCR/Nougat fusion) is still todo — RESEARCH.md:122 marks it "todo", docs/SESSION-2026-07-01-summary.md:140 queues it after pkg 2, and none of the 4 shipped specs in docs/superpowers/specs/ touch OCR/visuals (only a roadmap mention at 2026-07-01-treeseg-content-map-design.md:111). (2) No "unclippable transcript-only" rule exists anywhere in backend (grep for "unclippable" returns nothing outside RESEARCH.md:103-104 open Q2). (3) Empirical: I ran the CURRENT pipeline (all shipped packages) on the canonical video via `.venv/bin/python -m backend.eval.run_eval 8uNod-pxvXg` — result: n_clips=2 (not literally 0 anymore, thanks to the judge rework's best-partial keep rule at backend/pipeline/assemble/validate.py:381-385 which excludes visuals_sufficient from the hard core), but comprehension_rate=0.0, mean_judge_score=0.4, unresolved_reference_rate=1.0, prerequisite_gap_rate=1.0, rejections_repair=5, and the built structure has 0 edges — the class still collapses to unusable clips when visual perception is absent. (4) The eval harness can never exercise the fix: run_eval.py:56-66 (_maybe_perceive) requires a cached work/<id>/video.mp4 and never downloads, and work/8uNod-pxvXg/ has only audio.m4a+transcript.json, so the visual class is structurally unmeasurable in eval today. (5) The feed surface defaults to the transcript-only fast profile (config.py:169 FEED_DEFAULT_PROFILE="fast").

REFUTED parts of the claim: "the on-screen equations/text are never recovered" and "missing_visual failures are unrepairable" are FALSE for the production full-profile path. MULTIMODAL=1 by default (config.py:164); the production orchestrator downloads the video and runs perception (backend/orchestrator.py:143-171); the primary visual pass is Gemini keyframe vision (config.py:242 VISION_ENGINE="gemini_keyframes"), whose prompt explicitly transcribes on-screen text/equations/code VERBATIM (backend/pipeline/understand/vision.py:37-45), and OCR-off-by-default is a documented deliberate choice because Gemini captions beat glyph OCR on equations/handwriting (backend/pipeline/understand/ocr.py:1-5; local OCR merge exists and is env-enableable, perceive.py:108-114). Proof it works: cached work/dHjWVlfNraM/structure.json (physics/kinematics video with video.mp4 cached) contains 69 visual_events including 42 kind="equation" with verbatim formulas (e.g. "d = Xf - Xo"). Recovered visuals reach the quality loop: visual_summary (understand/models.py:159-167) is injected into the judge prompt as "ON-SCREEN TEXT" (assemble/validate.py:107), and missing_visual/visuals_sufficient failures trigger repair that pulls visually_depends_on graph units (validate.py:236-237, 249-250; edges built at understand/dependencies.py:80-104).

IMPACT — material, not cosmetic: a measured comprehension_rate of 0.0 on the canonical math video means the genre yields no usable clips whenever perception is absent (feed fast path, degraded runs, un-downloadable video, and ALL eval measurement), which is a direct coverage/comprehension-in-isolation loss. But since the production default path already performs equation recovery and visual-aware repair, the remaining work (OCR fusion as a supplement, the unclippable threshold, making the eval able to measure the class) is an improvement/verification gap rather than the total absence the claim describes — hence downgrade from high to medium. Key files: /Users/vincentfeng/Documents/practice/clips/backend/config.py, /Users/vincentfeng/Documents/practice/clips/backend/pipeline/understand/vision.py, /Users/vincentfeng/Documents/practice/clips/backend/pipeline/understand/ocr.py, /Users/vincentfeng/Documents/practice/clips/backend/pipeline/assemble/validate.py, /Users/vincentfeng/Documents/practice/clips/backend/eval/run_eval.py, /Users/vincentfeng/Documents/practice/clips/RESEARCH.md, /Users/vincentfeng/Documents/practice/clips/docs/SESSION-2026-07-01-summary.md.

verified_top.title: Comprehension affordances (context cards, 'watch first' hints, titles, quality signals) are computed but never shown to the viewer
verified_top.evidence: Audit [high/S] docs/clip-editing-robustness-audit.md:111-121 (cards/prereqs never rendered; verdict/warnings/final_quality stripped by _build_embed_clips); docs/SESSION-2026-07-01-summary.md:109-113 (pkg-2 scope A: 'both generated today but never shown' — not yet built). Confirmed: frontend/src/components/ClipCard.tsx has no context_card/prerequisite/quality rendering; backend/orchestrator.py _build_embed_clips whitelist unchanged.
verified_top.why_it_hurts: The pipeline's entire mechanism for isolation comprehension — the 'earlier in the video…' card and prerequisite hints — is paid for in LLM calls, shipped over the wire, and thrown away at render time. End users see strictly worse comprehension than the pipeline already achieved, and cannot triage which of their few clips are the dubious ones.
verified_top.fix_direction: Pkg-2 scope A (planned, unbuilt): expose verdict summary/warnings/final_quality/rejections in clip payloads; render cards as a preface, prerequisite chips, and per-clip quality badges.

verdict.corrected_status: high — the pipeline's only comprehension-in-isolation delivery mechanism (context card, prerequisites, titles) and all per-clip quality triage signals reach no user surface, despite being fully computed and (partly) already in the payload
verdict.reasoning: OPEN: backend generates context cards (backend/pipeline/assemble/context_card.py:43-63, one LLM call per clip), prerequisite hints (backend/pipeline/assemble/sequence.py:9-18), and final_quality/judge verdicts (backend/pipeline/assemble/__init__.py:122-163). _build_embed_clips (backend/orchestrator.py:45-68) ships title/context_card/prerequisite_clips (lines 62-66) but strips final_quality/verdict/warnings/rejections entirely. Frontend declares the fields (frontend/src/types.ts:14-16) but nothing renders them: ClipCard.tsx:41-58 shows only facet/timestamps/reason/export; ResultsGrid.tsx:4-11 just maps ClipCard; only a notes string surfaces (App.tsx:78). Exports don't burn the card in either (no context_card/title/drawtext refs in backend/pipeline/cut.py or export.py). NOT FIXED by shipped work: docs/superpowers/specs/ holds only integrity-chain, judge-rubric, treeseg, and quick-wins designs; quick-wins 'surfacing' is backend rejection-ledger relevance surfacing (2026-07-02-quick-wins-design.md:21), not UI. docs/SESSION-2026-07-01-summary.md:103-113 marks Pkg-2 scope A as 'NEXT; not yet spec'd'. IMPACT: material, not cosmetic — the card is the designed fallback for clips whose prerequisite units could not be included (context_card.py:44-46), so for exactly the hardest-to-understand clips the viewer sees nothing the mechanism produced; failed-judge best-partial clips ship unmarked and final_quality is stripped, so users cannot triage dubious clips. Fix is cheap (data already exists at orchestrator.py:64-66; quality fields need one whitelist addition). One precision correction to the mined claim: quality signals (verdict/warnings/final_quality) are stripped server-side (orchestrator.py:51-67) rather than 'shipped and thrown away'; only title/context_card/prereqs are shipped-and-ignored. End state identical: never shown.

verified_top.title: Best-partial path bypasses the comprehension floor: sub-threshold clips ship unmarked and the judge's score never feeds final_quality
verified_top.evidence: Audit [medium/S] docs/clip-editing-robustness-audit.md:309-313 (best-partial ignores min_score; understandable never consulted; final_quality has no verdict.score term so partials land ~0.8, above the 0.45 floor). Confirmed open: backend/pipeline/assemble/validate.py:377 best-partial hard-core gate still live; not addressed by pkg 1/3.
verified_top.why_it_hurts: Clips the judge scored well below 0.70 (even understandable=False) ship with no marker — they are the clips dragging measured comprehension to 0.333 while looking healthy in the payload, and the quality floor cannot catch them because the floor and the comprehension gate measure disjoint things.
verified_top.fix_direction: Audit proposal: blend verdict.score into completeness_score (e.g. 0.5*boolean-ratio + 0.5*score) or add a secondary floor for partials (score >= min_score - 0.15) plus a 'partial: true' flag surfaced downstream.

verdict.corrected_status: medium — best-partial gate still bypasses min_score with no partial flag, and verdict.score/understandable feed neither final_quality nor any downstream gate
verdict.reasoning: OPEN, confirmed by direct code read. (1) backend/pipeline/assemble/validate.py:377-380: best-partial returns any candidate passing only the 4 hard-core booleans (topic_identifiable, purpose_identifiable, source_grounded, all_references_resolved) — no min_score check, no understandable check, no flag set; contrast is_complete at validate.py:142-146 which requires v.score >= min_score (0.70). (2) understandable is write-only: set at validate.py:35,129, never read by any gate (grep over backend/). (3) verdict.score absent from scoring: backend/pipeline/assemble/scoring.py:8-18 (completeness = boolean pass-ratio − 0.05/failure_reason) and :43-48 (quality = 0.40*completeness+0.30*grounding+0.20*boundary+0.10*priority); a 4/5-boolean partial lands ~0.8 final_quality, above the 0.45 floor at assemble/__init__.py:155-157, so floor and comprehension gate measure disjoint things. (4) No 'partial' marker anywhere in backend/**/*.py (grep); post-merge re-judge at __init__.py:135-141 uses the same booleans-only gate. (5) Not fixed by shipped packages: quick-wins spec docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-02-quick-wins-design.md:13-73 covers 7 unrelated fixes; judge-rubric spec 2026-07-01-judge-rubric-design.md:52-58 only changed the outage-verdict path and explicitly kept the booleans-only best-partial gate; integrity-chain/TreeSeg specs untouched. IMPACT: material, not cosmetic — eval comprehension counts v.score >= threshold per clip (backend/eval/metrics.py:44-45), so sub-0.70 best-partials are by construction comprehension failures that the quality floor structurally cannot reject and that ship unmarked (no flag for eval/frontend to segregate); these are the clips depressing the 0.333 comprehension rate while showing healthy final_quality. Severity stays medium (not high) because the 4 hard-core booleans do reject the worst candidates (rejection path validate.py:381-388), so this is a gate-consistency hole plus missing marker rather than a fully open gate.

verified_top.title: Repair/closure span budget (300s) exceeds the physical cut cap (240s); post-judge shrinks still leave overclaiming metadata
verified_top.evidence: backend/config.py:123,213 (max_clip_duration_s=240 vs CLOSURE_MAX_SPAN_S=300, comment admits 'refine still caps to max_clip_duration_s'); audit [high/M] docs/clip-editing-robustness-audit.md:75-79 (repair adds result units at the END, exactly what capping chops; only a 0.10 boundary penalty); Post-IC backlog docs/clip-editing-robustness-audit.md:358-361 + docs/SESSION-2026-07-01-summary.md:130-133 (shrink-aware metadata repair, whisper-drift warning, merged grounding recompute — all deferred to pkg 2). Pkg 1 fixed grow-side truthfulness only.
verified_top.why_it_hurts: A clip that passed the judge precisely because repair appended the result can ship with the result cut off — the viewer watches a worked example with no answer. Trim/cap shrinks leave unit_ids overclaiming, so cards, prerequisite hints, and eval grounding describe content no longer in the clip.
verified_top.fix_direction: Audit proposal: clamp the repair/closure span budget to max_clip_duration_s so repair can't add content the snap will cut; shrink-aware unit_ids helper on trim/cap paths; recompute boundary/final quality after the Whisper pass (pkg-2 D backlog).

verdict.corrected_status: medium — confirmed open: closure/repair budget (300s) still exceeds the 240s cut cap with no clamp, capped clips are never re-judged or contract-rechecked, and true_contents is grow-only so trim/cap shrinks leave unit_ids/cards/grounding overclaiming; explicitly deferred to pkg 2 by the shipped quick-wins spec.
verdict.reasoning: REALITY (open): backend/config.py:123 (max_clip_duration_s=240) vs config.py:133 + config.py:213 (closure_max_span_s / CLOSURE_MAX_SPAN_S = 300, comment "refine still caps to max_clip_duration_s"). Closure builds candidates to the 300s budget (backend/pipeline/assemble/candidates.py:95) and the judge/repair loop grows within 300s (backend/pipeline/assemble/validate.py:328, used by _fill_contract at validate.py:312); no clamp to max_clip_duration_s exists anywhere in the repo. After judging, _snap_one pulls the end IN when over 240s (backend/pipeline/refine.py:88-101) with only a `capped_max_duration` warning worth a 0.10 boundary penalty (backend/pipeline/assemble/scoring.py:33); the post-snap hybrid re-judge covers ONLY `merged` specs (backend/pipeline/assemble/__init__.py:113-147) and check_contract is never re-run post-snap (sole call validate.py:347). Shrink overclaim confirmed: true_contents only ADDS units inside the span, never removes now-outside unit_ids (backend/pipeline/assemble/integrity.py:28-40; call-site comment scopes it to the grow direction, boundary_adapt.py:56-57); stale unit_ids feed context cards, prerequisite hints, and grounding (__init__.py:176-187). Not fixed by shipped work: quick-wins spec explicitly defers "shrink-aware metadata repair (pkg-2 backlog)" (docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-02-quick-wins-design.md:78); audit residual note docs/clip-editing-robustness-audit.md:359; backlog docs/SESSION-2026-07-01-summary.md:130. Pkg 1 (clip-integrity-chain) shipped grow-side truthfulness + merged-span re-judge only. IMPACT (material): repair's pull_result/pull_reasoning append outcome units at the END (validate.py:183-200 region) under the 300s budget — the 240s cap chops exactly that tail after the judge passed the full text, so a clip credited for having its result can ship without it (comprehension-in-isolation failure on the headline metric), while overclaiming unit_ids corrupt cards/hints/grounding. Bounded frequency (only spans in the 240-300s window, i.e., the config's own worked-problem case, config.py:123) keeps it medium rather than high; fix direction (clamp budget + shrink-aware true_contents + post-cap contract recheck) is cheap and directly quality-relevant.

verified_top.title: Contract/closure semantics unsound: three dead adapter knobs, position-blind precheck, greedy contract inlining starves graph closure
verified_top.evidence: Audit [medium/M+S] docs/clip-editing-robustness-audit.md:153-157 (repeatable / max_span_units / close_concept_prerequisites set by 8 adapters, zero consumers — re-verified by grep: no readers outside adapters/), :243-247 (check_contract ignores element position and anchor, so the NEXT problem's setup satisfies 'before'), :249-253 (document-order-greedy step-1 inlining; contract units consume max_extra_units so contract-heavy anchors get zero dependency closure).
verified_top.why_it_hurts: Clips splice in the wrong problem statement (a mislabeled setup from 10 minutes earlier), pass the deterministic precheck while missing their actual required element, and contract-heavy anchors ship with no prerequisite closure — producing both judge rejections (feeding the 7.7/video) and shipped clips that confuse viewers in exactly the ways the contracts were designed to prevent.
verified_top.fix_direction: Audit proposals: implement the three flags (nearest-only for non-repeatable 'within', thread max_span_units into ClosureBudget, gate the requires-walk on close_concept_prerequisites); pass anchor/order into check_contract; sort required elements nearest-first and give graph closure guaranteed headroom.

verdict.corrected_status: medium — all three defects verified open in current code (dead knobs, position-blind check_contract gating a position-aware fixer, step-1 contract units consuming the step-2 unit budget), and they unconditionally degrade prerequisite closure and precheck reliability on contract-bearing anchors
verdict.reasoning: (1) Dead knobs: repeatable (backend/adapters/base.py:40), close_concept_prerequisites (base.py:48), max_span_units (base.py:49) are set by 8 adapters (lecture.py:67,70,74; review.py:39; base.py:99,102; tutorial/coding/recipe/news/story) but grep over backend/ finds zero readers outside adapters/. closure.py:79-80 pulls all role-matches within ±5 regardless of repeatable; closure.py:74-78 picks exactly one for before/after; ClosureBudget (closure.py:22-25) is built only from config/settings at candidates.py:95 (max_span_units never threaded); step-2 graph walk (closure.py:113-141) ignores close_concept_prerequisites. (2) Position-blind precheck: check_contract (contracts.py:21-32) has no anchor/order parameter and tests only role-set membership, so a 'before' element like problem_statement (base.py:98) is satisfied by a unit after the anchor; the false pass skips the position-aware _fill_contract (gated at validate.py:347-348; fixer at validate.py:298-307). (3) Budget starvation: closure.py:101 sorts required contract units by document order (earliest-first consumes span budget); they are inlined without a unit cap (closure.py:102-111) but count in step 2's fit test closure.py:131 (len(inline)-1 < max_extra_units, default 6 per config.py:211), so a lecture worked-example within-element pulling ±5 units exhausts the budget and every graph prerequisite goes referential — zero dependency closure. Refutation attempt failed: none of the shipped packages touch this — grep for the knobs/check_contract across docs/superpowers/specs/ and plans/ (2026-07-01 clip-integrity-chain, judge-rubric, treeseg-content-map; 2026-07-02 quick-wins) returns nothing. Impact is material: unmet prerequisites are not a hard judge gate (best-partial path in validate.py), so starved clips ship with missing prerequisite context (comprehension-in-isolation), and precheck false-passes feed judge rejections and incomplete best-partials (yield). The 'wrong problem from 10 minutes earlier' scenario is conditional on role mislabeling, but the ±5 repeatable-blind pull and budget starvation are unconditional on contract-bearing anchors — medium, not high, because fixes are deterministic and the worst-case splice requires labeling errors.
